summary: Core functionality (secret classification, config loading, report generation) is well-covered with good fixtures and clear test organization. However, significant gaps exist: (1) the CLI main() function is never tested despite being the entry point; (2) fixture files referenced in run_tests.py (all_ok_config.json, all_expired_config.json) are missing, preventing integration tests from running; (3) markdown format validation is superficial compared to JSON; (4) integration test assertions use brittle lambda string matching; (5) missing edge cases like negative rotation policies, special characters in names, and very old secrets. The test suite would catch basic regressions but fails to validate CLI argument parsing and has critical dependencies on missing fixture files.
